name: Uppama
recipeIngredient: 1 small Potato, peeled and cut into 1/4-inch dice
1 cup Cream of wheat (15 minute variety)
recipeInstructions:
1. Pour the oil into a medium or large skillet and set over medium heat.
2. When the oil is hot, add the asafetida, mustard seeds, red pepper, chana dal, and urad dal. Stir until dals turn reddish in color.
3. Add onion, cabbage, potato, peas, green chili, and ginger to the pan. Increase heat to medium-high and cook for about 2 minutes, stirring continuously.
4. Cover skillet, reduce heat to low, and simmer until vegetables are done (about 5-6 minutes).
5. Uncover and mix in salt. Increase heat to medium and add cream of wheat. Cook for 5 minutes, stirring the mixture and ensuring it doesn't brown.
6. Decrease heat to low. Add boiling water, a couple of tablespoons at a time, stirring after each addition and allowing water to be absorbed before adding more (more water can be used if a moister uppama is desired).
7. Continue stirring and cooking over low heat for 10 more minutes.
8. Garnish with minced cilantro before serving.
